checks.a_analysis: The output contains only information found in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. Document titles, alt text, and table summaries are within the scope of acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility.

All contact information in the output, including the Conference Bridge number (888-808-6929) and meeting ID (8153047#), matches the source document exactly. Addresses for meeting locations in Raleigh are also correct.

The output includes content from both page 1 and page 2 of the source document. All agenda items from 1 to 13, the consent agenda financial details, the committee meeting schedule, and the footer logistics are present.

The agenda topics and presenters are split into two tables in the output, corresponding to their presentation across the two pages of the source. The total number of agenda items (1-13 plus 'Call to Order' and 'Roll Call') is accurately represented across these tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The complex financial data under agenda item 4 (Consent Agenda) was audited line-by-line. Every fund balance and disbursement amount for November and December 2018 for the NG 911 Fund, CMRS Fund, PSAP Fund, and Grant Fund is present and correct in the output.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the output preserves the meaning and technical data of the source document. Minor changes in layout (such as consolidating sub-items of the consent agenda into a table cell) are acceptable reformatting for semantic structure.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly handles the document content without duplicating sections or paragraphs. The page headers/footers ('NC 911 Board Agenda 20190118 ● Page 1 of 2') are excluded from the main sections, and each agenda item appears only once.

The heading hierarchy is logical. H2 is used for the document title and main header. H3 is used for the distinct meeting sections at the end of the document. While an H1 is missing from the elements list, the transition from H2 to H3 is correct and follows a nested structure.

The agenda is presented as a table in the source document. The output splits this into two table objects (due to the page break) and a paragraph. Crucially, item 4's sub-points 'g)' and 'h)' are placed in a paragraph element (element index 9) between the two tables. This orphans these items from the table's 3-column structure, losing their association with the 'Presenter' column (Pokey Harris).
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 4 contains sub-points a) through h), all associated with presenter 'Pokey Harris' in a 3-column table layout spanning pages 1 and 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output places sub-points a-f in element 8 (table row) and sub-points g-h in element 9 (paragraph), breaking the table structure.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document. Even though the table structure is fragmented, the logical sequence of agenda items 1 through 13 is preserved.

The source contains several lists. While the top-level list (1-13) is rendered as table rows, the sub-list of names under item g) (i. Vicki Callicutt through v. Tracy Trogdon) is flattened into a single paragraph block using newline characters rather than being structured as a semantic list element.
checks.b_evidence_source: g) Peer Reviewer Approvals... i. Vicki Callicutt ii. Megan Daub iii. Greg Dotson iv. Mark Reavis v. Tracy Trogdon
checks.b_evidence_output:

i. Vicki Callicutt
ii. Megan Daub
iii. Greg Dotson
iv. Mark Reavis
v. Tracy Trogdon
h) Orthoimagery September, October, and November 2018 Reports"

checks.a_analysis: The output captures the text for the WebEx link and phone number. While they are not marked as functional hyperlinks in the JSON, they are not 'missing' or 'broken' as the textual information is preserved.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output significantly alters the visual organization of the agenda items. In the source PDF, the agenda is a single, cohesive list from items 1 through 13. In the HTML, this is split into two separate tables ('Agenda Topics and Presenters' and 'Agenda Topics and Presenters Continued'). Crucially, sub-items 4g and 4h are placed as plain text between these two tables, losing their visual context as part of item 4. Additionally, the complex structure of item 4 (a-f), which contains aligned financial data in the PDF, is collapsed into a single, dense paragraph within one table cell in the HTML, drastically changing the visual flow and legibility of the information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF shows a continuous list of agenda items (1-13) with item 4 sub-points (a-h) clearly organized beneath it. Item 4 sub-points a-f include financial figures aligned to the right of the topic text.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML renders the agenda as two separate tables. Items 4g and 4h are rendered as plain text between the two tables, breaking the visual sequence. Sub-items 4a-4f are merged into a single text block within one table cell.

checks.a_analysis: The rendering of the data in item 4 within the HTML table is visually broken. In the original document, the NG 911, CMRS, PSAP, and Grant funds are presented with sub-rows for 'Fund Balance' and 'Disbursement' (or 'Encumbered'), with currency values neatly aligned on the right. The HTML conversion flattens all of this structured data into a single continuous string of text within a single cell. This makes the financial data nearly impossible to parse visually, as the labels and values are run together without any tabular structure or alignment.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF, Item 4b-e show financial data (Fund Balance, Disbursement) in a tabular format where labels are on the left and currency values are aligned on the right in distinct rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML, these financial lines are flattened into a single paragraph inside the 'Topic' cell for Item 4: 'November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858 November 2018 Disbursement -$15,088...'. All row and column visual structure is lost.
checks.b_evidence_location: First table under 'Agenda Topics and Presenters', row 4.

checks.a_analysis: The main 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo from the top of the document is correctly rendered at the beginning of the HTML page.
No visual content duplication was observed in the HTML output. Each section of the agenda and meeting information appears once.
